conclusion: MCM6 enhancer variant (historically reported as -13910T on the reverse-strand numbering) homozygous for the derived allele. Associated with sustained lactase (LCT) expression into adulthood and has been linked to lactose tolerance in populations of European ancestry.

Heterozygous carrier of the derived MCM6 enhancer allele. Lactase persistence is inherited as an autosomal dominant trait, so heterozygotes are generally associated with lactase persistence and lactose tolerance similar to homozygotes, though with somewhat more variable penetrance reported in the literature.

Homozygous ancestral genotype. Associated with the typical post-weaning decline in lactase expression (adult-type hypolactasia), which may contribute to lactose intolerance symptoms after dairy consumption. This is the ancestral human condition rather than a disease state, and many carriers remain asymptomatic.

MCM6 intronic variant (historically reported as -22018G on the reverse-strand numbering) homozygous for the derived allele. This variant sits in strong linkage disequilibrium with rs4988235 and is commonly used as a tag marker for the same lactase persistence haplotype; it has been associated with lactose tolerance but is not itself established as the functionally causal site.

Heterozygous carrier of the derived allele at this linked marker. Because this variant tags the same dominant persistence haplotype as rs4988235, heterozygotes have been associated with lactase persistence, though this SNP alone is a proxy rather than the proven causal variant.

Homozygous ancestral genotype at this linked marker. Consistent with the typical lactase non-persistence haplotype and may contribute to lactose intolerance symptoms, mirroring the rs4988235 ancestral genotype it is co-inherited with.
phenotype: Lactase non-persistence / possible lactose intolerance (linked marker)
